risks.title: Header block placement disrupts file parsing
risks.mitigation: Place header in the file-level JSDoc block position (after shebang + file-level comment, before imports). Verify existing tests still pass after header insertion.

risks.title: Test mocking of dynamic imports is fragile
risks.mitigation: Avoid testing dynamic import paths; test parseArgs() and static logic in isolation using argument arrays. This pattern mirrors populate-cli.test.ts.

risks.title: rag-status vectorsPath uses legacy sqlite name for what is now a JSON file
risks.mitigation: Test against actual path constant (CODEREF_SQLITE_PATH env var path). No behavior change needed — this is a known naming mismatch documented in comments.

candidates.reason: Public CLI entry point — zero semantic header coverage
Public CLI, complexity=54, zero header + zero tests
Public CLI, complexity=48, zero header + zero tests
Public CLI, complexity=35, zero header + zero tests

decision_records.rationale: rag-search.ts main() requires a live embedding provider for any meaningful test. The parseArgs() function is nearly identical to rag-index.ts. Adding fragile mocked tests for nearly-duplicate code is low value. Header coverage is the high-leverage item here.
decision_records.outcome: Only rag-index-cli.test.ts and rag-status-cli.test.ts are created. rag-search header is added.

decision_records.decision: populate.ts gets header only, no new tests
decision_records.rationale: populate-cli.test.ts already has 5 meaningful test cases covering the main execution paths. Adding redundant tests would violate the 'no over-engineering' constraint.

decision_records.decision: Header block format: @capability, @layer, @exports inline in file-level JSDoc
decision_records.rationale: Matches existing header format in well-documented files across the codebase. The semantic header is a JSDoc block at the file level.
decision_records.outcome: Headers placed after the shebang + existing file-level JSDoc comment at line 1-10
